text.title: A/B Testing Implementation Failure Modes
text.summary: 3 antipatterns observed in real deployments. Each pairs symptom + root cause + concrete fix.

antipattern.symptom: Same user fires 12 exposures per session; conversion rate looks 12× lower than reality.
antipattern.fix: Add a per-request seen-set keyed by experiment_id; emit exposure on first observation only.

antipattern.root-cause: Author used naive normal CI for proportion.

antipattern.symptom: Primary metric flat; team slices by browser and announces 'Safari users converted'.
antipattern.root-cause: Multiple-comparisons via segment slicing without correction.
antipattern.fix: Forbid post-hoc segmentation as a winner-declaration mechanism; report segments but require pre-registration for them to count.
